crime.value: Witchcraft and making a pact with the devil to have power over women
crime.quote: From hell came the tidings that by horrible sanctions I had made a pact with the devil to have power over women

victim.value: Urbain Grandier, a priest who was controversial and had both supporters and enemies in the town
victim.quote: there was such difference of opinion that discussion on the subject between those who were on his side

closed_world.value: The town of Loudun and its religious institutions, particularly the convent
closed_world.quote: there was great excitement in the little town of Loudun

culprit_and_motive.value: Religious authorities and enemies seeking revenge against Grandier
culprit_and_motive.quote: Like her, I too fell a victim to revenge

resolution.value: Grandier was executed by burning at the stake based on testimony from supposed demonic possession
resolution.quote: In the trial which delivered me to torture and the stake, The demon who accused me invented and suggested the crime

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple suspects with varying motives: nuns claiming possession (Sister Jeanne, Sister Claire), ecclesiastical authorities seeking Grandier's downfall, political enemies exploiting religious accusations, and townspeople divided into supporters and opponents
suspect_architecture.quote: there was such difference of opinion that discussion on the subject between those who were on his side

red_herring.value: Demonic possession as supernatural explanation deliberately staged to mask human conspiracy - nuns' theatrical exorcisms and claimed supernatural knowledge used to frame Grandier for witchcraft
red_herring.quote: the demon who accused me invented and suggested the crime, And his testimony was the only proof against me

clue_fairness.value: The nuns' failed supernatural claims when tested - unable to correctly identify Grandier's location when asked by investigators, exposing the fraud
clue_fairness.quote: She answered without the slightest hesitation that he was walking with the bailiff in the church of Sainte-Croix

social_world.value: Complex ecclesiastical and civil power structure: bailiff vs. ecclesiastical authorities, Parliament vs. Church jurisdiction, Bishop of Poitiers' selective silence, attorney-general's jurisdictional limitations
social_world.quote: the attorney-general replied that the matter being entirely ecclesiastical the Parliament was not competent

alibi.value: Grandier's verifiable whereabouts used to expose the nuns' false supernatural knowledge - when they claim he's at Sainte-Croix church, investigators find him elsewhere with the bailiff
alibi.quote: finding the church empty, went on to the palace, and saw the bailiff presiding at a court

media_and_audience.value: Public divided into opposing factions, persecution inspiring journalists and poets, competing narratives of guilt vs. innocence, supernatural vs. conspiracy, with townspeople literally shutting doors or opening them based on allegiance
media_and_audience.quote: some withdrew into their houses and shut their doors and darkened their windows, as if it were a day of mourning

investigator_wound.value: The bailiff's institutional vulnerability - torn between duty to investigate fairly and fear that ecclesiastical authorities will nullify his procedures due to jurisdictional limitations
investigator_wound.quote: he was afraid that the ecclesiastical authorities would nullify his procedure, on the ground that the convent

_meta.title: ebook alexandre dumas pere urbain grandier
